title: [feraligatr] Battle Guide
summary: A [water] physical setup sweeper whose [sheer-force] ability converts [liquidation], [crunch], and [ice-punch] into 1.3x boosted finishers after a [dragon-dance].
strengths: [sheer-force] boosts every secondary-effect move by 30 percent
[dragon-dance] solves the Speed tier problem in one turn
Broad coverage pool — Ghost, Dragon, Ground, and Psychic all covered
weaknesses: 78 Speed is too slow without a [dragon-dance] up
2x Grass and Electric weaknesses threaten the setup turn
Priority attackers easily interrupt the setup

recommendedItems.reason: Boosts [water] STAB damage with no drawback
Absorbs one status move to secure the setup turn

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[feraligatr] is a [water]-type physical setup sweeper whose entire plan revolves around the [sheer-force] hidden ability plus [dragon-dance]. Base 105 Attack and 78 Speed look mediocre at first, but [sheer-force] boosts every secondary-effect move ([liquidation], [crunch], [ice-punch]) by 30 percent, and a single dance turns those into game-ending finishers. In the Champions format [feraligatr] is a reliable late-game sweeper that closes out matches once the opposing walls have been weakened.

## Strengths
[sheer-force] is the damage scaler. [liquidation], [crunch], and [ice-punch] all qualify, so [feraligatr]'s effective power is considerably higher than its raw stats suggest. [dragon-dance] solves the Speed tier in one turn. The coverage trio of [water], [dark], and Ice covers almost every wall in the format, making [feraligatr] difficult to switch into after setup.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 78 Speed is the main structural issue. 2x Grass and Electric weaknesses mean any special attacker running those types threatens to end the setup turn. Priority attackers — extreme-speed, aqua-jet from Scizor-style users.

## Recommended Moveset
[liquidation] is the locked [water] STAB because [sheer-force] boosts it and it has a defense-lowering secondary that's a meaningful threat mid-sweep. [crunch] is the mandatory [dark] move for Ghost and Psychic walls. [ice-punch] covers Grass, Dragon, and Ground attackers that would otherwise wall the [water] STAB. [dragon-dance] fills the fourth slot. earthquake is an alternative for Electric coverage if the team needs it.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[mystic-water] is a clean [water] STAB booster with no drawbacks and doesn't lock moves, making it the default for the four-attack set. [lum-berry] is the alternate that absorbs one status attempt and guarantees the setup turn. The ability must be [sheer-force]. Tera [water] maxes [liquidation] damage, and Tera [dark] turns the setup mid-sweep into a [crunch]-oriented cleaner with extra coverage resistance.

## Teammates & Synergy
The biggest structural need is Grass and Electric coverage. A slow pivot creates the entry turns [feraligatr] needs to dance, and an intimidate user on the back line reduces the priority pressure that would otherwise interrupt the setup. Hazard removal also matters for [feraligatr]'s bulk over a long game.

## Play Patterns
[feraligatr] wants a late-game setup slot. Enter on a predicted [water]-weak switch, dance on the swap turn, and start chaining coverage attacks. Use [crunch] against Ghost and Psychic walls, [ice-punch] for Dragons and Grasses, and [liquidation] for everything neutral. Save the Tera for the moment a Grass or Electric move is confirmed.
